Our Story

About Us

Discover the mission, vision, and the compassionate team behind PhilCHAN Cebu's commitment to faith-based health services.

Who is PhilCHAN Cebu?

The Philippine Catholic HIV/AIDS Network Inc. (PhilCHAN) is a Church-based response to the HIV/AIDS epidemic, formed to provide care, education, and advocacy grounded in Catholic social teaching. The Cebu chapter serves the Central Visayas region with free testing, counseling, and community outreach - treating every person with the dignity owed to them, regardless of status or circumstance.

We work alongside parishes, the Department of Health, and local government units to close gaps in access to testing and reduce the stigma that keeps people from seeking care.

1992
Founded by the Catholic Bishops' Conference of the Philippines
30+
Years supporting people living with HIV/AIDS in the Visayas
100%
Free testing, counseling, and pastoral care - no cost, ever
Our Purpose

Guided by Faith & Care

Mission

To champion the human rights of all people living with HIV/AIDS, ensure comprehensive access to prevention, treatment, care, and support, and to break down the walls of stigma through the teachings and compassion of the Catholic Church.

Care and Compassion in Action

Vision

A society fully educated about HIV/AIDS where every individual, regardless of status, lives with dignity, experiences the unconditional love of the community, and has full access to the health resources they need to thrive.

A Future Built on Dignity

Our Leadership

A multidisciplinary team of medical, pastoral, and community leaders working together.

President, PhilCHAN

Rev. Fr. Rodolfo V. Cancino Jr., MI

Executive Secretary, CBCP Episcopal Commission on Healthcare (CBCP-ECHC) & Vicar Provincial, Ministers of the Infirm (Camillians).
